name: Adobe Firefly API
what_it_does: Adobe Firefly generative AI API for creating images, vectors, and creative assets from text prompts, with enterprise-grade content credentials and commercial-safe training data.
use_cases: Automated creative asset generation for marketing pipelines
Brand-consistent image generation with style references
Vector and illustration creation from text prompts
not_for: Real-time interactive applications requiring sub-second latency
High-volume low-cost image generation (expensive per-call pricing)
Non-commercial hobbyist use without Creative Cloud subscription
best_when: Enterprise pipelines needing commercially safe generative images with Adobe Content Credentials and brand consistency controls.
avoid_when: Budget-constrained projects or when simple background removal or basic edits are sufficient.

auth.notes: Requires OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow with Adobe IMS. Access tokens expire after 24 hours. Client ID and secret required. More complex than typical API key auth.

free_tier_limits.credits: 25 free generative credits/month with free tier

pricing.notes: Generative credit system; enterprise pricing negotiated. Free tier very limited. CC subscribers get monthly credits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP error codes with JSON body. OAuth errors can be cryptic. Credit exhaustion returns 429 with quota context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys; each generation call creates a new asset
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth token expiry (24h) requires token refresh logic in long-running agents
Credit exhaustion returns 429 which conflicts with rate limit semantics
Async generation jobs require polling — no webhook for completion
Content moderation rejections return 400 with minimal explanation
Enterprise API access requires separate approval from consumer Creative Cloud

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials is stronger than simple API keys. Adobe IMS provides enterprise-grade identity. Content Credentials (C2PA) embedded in outputs. No webhook signature verification needed (no webhooks). Short-lived access tokens are a security positive.
